title: Deuteronomy: Community Life
objective: Deepen biblical-theological understanding of deuteronomy: community life and its place in redemptive history.

required_passages.text: Selected key passages for this unit segment, read in full during lesson delivery.

segments.audio_script: Today we study deuteronomy: community life and trace God's covenant faithfulness.
Listen to the assigned passage and note covenant themes, divine actions, and human response.
We place this text in its historical setting, canonical location, and literary flow.
We analyze theological meaning, covenant development, and connections to the wider biblical story.
Key themes include God's holiness, covenant faithfulness, judgment and mercy, and redemptive purpose.

Review question: What did the worshipper recite when presenting the firstfruits offering in Deuteronomy 26? Answer: a historical recital beginning with My father was a wandering Aramean, summarizing God's saving acts from Abraham through the exodus and entry into the land.
segments.correct_answer: a recital of Israel's history from Abraham's wandering through the exodus to entry into the land
acceptable_variants: my father was a wandering aramean — the salvation history confession
a creedal summary of Israel's story from patriarchs to the land
the historical confession beginning with the wandering aramean

segments.audio_script: Carry this lesson into prayer, worship, and careful Scripture reading as we continue the course.

vocabulary.definition: The first and best portion of harvest brought to God in Deuteronomy 26; the act acknowledged God's ownership of the land and Israel's dependence on his provision
The description of Jacob in Deuteronomy 26:5; Israel's identity begins not with triumph but with a foreigner's journey, establishing that their nationhood is entirely God's gift
